notation_table.H[k]: Channel frequency response at subcarrier k
notation_table.N[k]: Complex Gaussian noise at subcarrier k
notation_table.\\hat{H}_{\\text{LS}}[k_p]: LS channel estimate at pilot position k_p
notation_table.\\mathbf{h}_t: Channel vector at diffusion timestep t
notation_table.a(\\mathbf{z}): Predicted amplitude from Phase Interaction Layer
notation_table.\\phi(\\mathbf{z}): Predicted phase from Phase Interaction Layer

theoretical_analysis:
**Computational Complexity**: The forward diffusion process requires $O(K)$ operations per timestep. The U-Net backbone with 3 down/up blocks has complexity $O(K \cdot C^2)$ where $C \in \{128, 256, 512\}$ is the channel dimension at each scale. The Phase Interaction Layer adds negligible overhead with $O(K)$ operations for the amplitude/phase composition. Total inference complexity is $O(T \cdot K \cdot C^2) \approx O(10^8)$ FLOPs for $T=500$ steps.

**Memory Requirements**: The model stores approximately 180K parameters (U-Net: ~170K, Phase Interaction heads: ~10K). During training, memory scales as $O(B \cdot K \cdot C)$ for batch size $B$, requiring ~2GB for $B=64$.

**Convergence Analysis**: The cosine noise schedule ensures $\bar{\alpha}_T \approx 0$, making $\mathbf{h}_T \approx \mathcal{CN}(\mathbf{0}, \mathbf{I})$ for sufficient noise saturation. The reverse process converges to the data distribution under standard DDPM assumptions, with the Phase Interaction Layer preserving the complex manifold structure through its amplitude-phase factorization.

**Pilot Consistency**: The RePaint-style pilot replacement maintains exact consistency at observed positions while allowing the diffusion model to denoise and interpolate the remaining subcarriers. This creates a constrained optimization problem that leverages both the learned prior and the observations.

evaluation_metrics.name: NMSE
evaluation_metrics.description: Normalized Mean Square Error in dB, measuring the relative channel estimation error across all subcarriers

evaluation_metrics.name: BER
evaluation_metrics.description: Bit Error Rate after Zero-Forcing equalization using the estimated channel, where $\hat{X}[k] = Y[k] / \hat{H}[k]$ and $N_b$ is the total number of transmitted bits

evaluation_metrics.name: Phase Error
evaluation_metrics.description: Average wrapped phase error across subcarriers, measuring the phase tracking accuracy of the estimator

baseline_methods: LS estimator: Direct pilot division $\hat{H}_{\text{LS}}[k_p] = Y[k_p]/X[k_p]$ followed by linear interpolation
MMSE estimator: $\hat{\mathbf{h}}_{\text{MMSE}} = \mathbf{R}_{hh}(\mathbf{R}_{hh} + \sigma^2/\text{SNR} \cdot \mathbf{I})^{-1} \hat{\mathbf{h}}_{\text{LS}}$ with perfect channel statistics
Real-valued U-Net denoiser: Identical U-Net architecture processing [Re, Im] channels but with standard 2-channel output (no Phase Interaction Layer)
DNN denoiser: 3-layer feedforward network with ReLU activations directly mapping $\hat{\mathbf{h}}_{\text{LS}}$ to refined estimate
